next_step_text: 16/02/26: Confidence level 35% February 13, 2026: Caroline had a call with Global Head of Procurement and tender will start in April - so have to check back in - timline is also a bit delayed cause of the S4Hana project which has started 7/11/2025: IT/Implementation session onsite to see if allowing early use of Source-to-Contract and Spend Analysis modules before full P2P integration. Implementat

buyer_dependent_items.text: Issue the tender and share its timeline and requirements to Zycus

competitive_position.summary: A two-vendor formal tender in which Zycus is the advantaged incumbent-of-engagement after 12 months of requirement shaping (David, 12 Oct 2025). Ivalua is the strongest current peer threat, named by David on 1 Aug 2025 as a desired solution class, though not confirmed as the second tender vendor. Coupa and SAP Ariba are dormant market references only (no buyer lean toward either). No new competitor evidence surfaced this sweep, so prior threat levels are carried forward unchanged; the live risk is not a preferred rival but price/feature parity being introduced by the formal tender itself.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.quote: There is a need to consider the potential of an FTE or a fractional FTE to enhance procurement excellence, along with a desire for a solution like Ivalua.
competitors.how_we_win: Depth of engagement and requirements shaping over 12 months; make the tender response mirror every workshop output (phased go-live, iSaaS Lite, AI contract review, EU data residency) so Zycus reads purpose-built and the peer looks generic.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: Avoma call 1 Aug 2025, mentioned as market reference
competitors.how_we_win: Not confirmed as shortlisted; treat as dormant unless the buyer raises it again.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: The world that they use Ariba, remember?
competitors.source: Avoma call 1 Aug 2025, reference to another customer, not Forbo's shortlist
competitors.how_we_win: Not confirmed as shortlisted; treat as dormant. If it is the second vendor, lead with Zycus end-to-end integration (iSaaS) and native AI vs Ariba complexity.

champion_strength.source: SF Next_Step 12 Oct 2025; Avoma calls Jul-Nov 2025; Task 5 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: David GALLO is a strong, active champion who built the business case, secured budget, and shaped the two-vendor tender in Zycus's favour. He is not at risk of switching, but he has not been re-engaged on a working session since 7 Nov 2025 and his latest touch is a light event attendance, so the relationship needs re-activation before the tender opens.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority is confirmed but the economic buyer is not named or personally engaged by the relevant stakeholder value case went to the CPO (25 Aug 2025) and David presented it to the CFO (26 Aug 2025), and budget was approved (2 Oct 2025), so an EB exists and has said yes to funding. On 13 Feb 2026 Caroline noted a call with the relevant stakeholder of Procurement, indicating a senior procurement owner above David. No Global Head of Procurement is on the Salesforce contact roles or on any recorded call, so Zycus has reached the EB only through David. Mapping and getting a direct line to that budget owner ahead of the April tender is the ranked move.

recommended_moves: Book a working call with David to confirm the April tender is live and get the written timeline.
Send David two or three named implementation partners who can run project and change management.
Deliver refreshed iSaaS Lite versus Advanced integration quotes with an IT-FTE resource plan.
